avalancheActivity.comment: Above the tree line there is a moderate avalanche risk (level 2). The soft new fallen snow was transported by strong westerly to north-westerly winds and fresh snowdrift accumulations formed, especially in the north, east and south aspects. Adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls, drift snow packs can be triggered as slab avalanches in some places by individual winter sports enthusiasts. Occasional avalanches are also possible in forest aisles. There is still a persistent weak layer problem on shady slopes at high altitudes. Avalanches can occasionally tear through angular weak layers and reach medium size.
avalancheActivity.highlights: Pay attention to fresh, prone to triggering wind slab.

snowpackStructure.comment: Above an altitude of around 1000 metres, another 10 to 20 cm of new fallen snow has been brought in by strong north-westerly winds. The fresh drift snow packs have deposited on decomposed snow or older drift snow layers and are often prone to triggering. Underneath, the old snowpack is unfavourably structured in places with crusts and faceted crystals. There are weak layers both at the transitions to the wind slab and within the old snowpack.

tendency.highlights:
A Mediterranean low will approach from the south on Thursday. During the afternoon, it will start to snow above around 1000 metres. During the night to Friday, it may snow heavily down to the valleys. Around 10 cm of new fallen snow will accumulate.
The avalanche risk remains moderate in the Ybbstal Alps above the tree line.

weatherForecast.comment: There will be a few snow showers on Wednesday night, resulting in around 5 cm of new fallen snow. The snowfall level is in the valleys. The snow showers will subside in the morning. In the morning, the clouds will soften and visibility will improve significantly. In the afternoon, some clouds will pass over the summit and the sun will only appear occasionally. In the morning, there will still be strong winds from the north-west with peak winds of between 30 and 60 km/h in exposed areas. The wind will weaken during the course of the day. The temperature at an altitude of 2000 metres will be between -11 and -8 degrees. The temperature at an altitude of 1500 metres is between -8 and -4 degrees.

avalancheActivity.comment: With the strong north-westerly wind, thin pillows of wind drifted snow have formed, which can be triggered as small slab avalanches in a few places with little additional load. In shady gullies and bowls, tearing through weak layers of old snow is possible, but avalanches usually remain small. The risk of entrainment and falling outweighs the risk of burial. Hardness or icy surfaces were covered by little new fallen snow and are sometimes difficult to recognise.
avalancheActivity.highlights: Low avalanche danger, but still isolated avalanche prone locations in gullies and bowls.

snowpackStructure.comment: At high altitudes, there is a considerable amount of older and mostly well-settled wind slab in some places in gullies and bowls. However, the snow thickness is rapidly decreasing distant from ridgelines. The new fallen snow of the last few days has also been transported. In the north, east and south aspects, thin pillows of wind drifted snow have formed in gullies and bowls, while windward areas are often icy and snow-poor. Below 1000 metres, a thin snowpack has formed on mostly bare ground.

tendency.highlights:
A Mediterranean low will approach from the south on Thursday. During the afternoon, it will start to snow above around 1000 metres. During the night to Friday, it may snow heavily down to the valleys. More than 10 cm of new fallen snow is expected.
The avalanche risk will remain low on Thursday.

weatherForecast.comment: There will be a few unproductive snow showers during the night into Wednesday, which will subside completely in the morning. The snowfall level will be in the valleys. Afterwards, the clouds will soften and it will be quite sunny. In the afternoon, dense clouds at higher altitudes. In the morning, strong to gale-force winds from the northwest with wind peaks between 60 and 100 km/h in exposed locations. The wind will weaken during the afternoon. The temperature at an altitude of 2000 metres will be between -11 and -8 degrees. The temperature at an altitude of 1500 metres will be between -8 and -5 degrees.

ALBINA.generalHeadline: Be aware of prone to triggering wind slab, especially in gullies and bowls.
